definition: The AFLFast FAST n_fuzz rarity schedule damps mutation energy for frequently-visited coverage buckets, starving deeply-structured seeds of per-seed mutation budget before the target branch can be resolved; minimizer's frequency-agnostic calibrated energy avoids this throttling.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: AFLFast FAST schedule multiplies energy by n_fuzz log2, damping frequently-hit (saturated) hash-lookup paths to ~0.4x, starving structured DTD seeds of mutation budget; minimizer's calibrated energy is frequency-agnostic and avoids this starvation.
signature.one_line: AFLFast n_fuzz damping starves saturated hash-lookup path seeds

analysis.summary_one_line: Resolving the name3 pointer-identity check requires a deeply-nested DTD that interns and re-looks-up the same name3 string so dict pointers alias.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same deep DTD structure reaching xmlHashLookup3 with an aliasing dict-interned name3, requiring sustained mutation pressure on the structured corpus seeds.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None (energy from intrinsic calibration only), so the structured DTD seeds keep earning mutations regardless of how often their coverage region was hit, resolving 9/10.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: fast is identical except its FAST AFLFast schedule multiplies energy by an n_fuzz log2 factor that damps frequently-hit (saturated) paths to 0.4x; the hash-lookup path is exercised often, so fast starves the very seeds that could build the deep structure (resolved 2/10, blocked 8/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: the AFLFast rarity power schedule (fast) reweights energy by region hit-frequency and damps the saturated hash-lookup path; minimizer's frequency-agnostic calibrated energy avoids this starvation — the aflfast_rarity delta is what separates them.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a pointer-identity comparison of name3 inside xmlHashLookup3 on the dict path.
The comparison sits on the dict-interned branch, where equality is pointer identity established by dict interning.
The branch is reached via deep DTD parsing (DOCTYPE / entity / attribute-list-decl call chain).
minimizer beats naive on the calibrated_energy delta, resolving 9/10 vs naive 0/10.
minimizer derives per-seed mutation energy from intrinsic calibration which naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often
a region has been hit

evidence_trail.claim: naive runs the havoc+token stack in a plain StdMutationalStage with no I2S and no calibrated energy.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: fast is identical to minimizer except the FAST AFLFast power schedule.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore `fast`
vs `minimizer`, not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: FAST schedule damps saturated paths to 0.4x energy via the n_fuzz factor.
minimizer beats fast on the aflfast_rarity delta (9/10 vs 2/10).
grimoire beats cmplog on the grimoire_structural delta, resolving 10/10 with hitcount 1004.
grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage plus structural Grimoire mutators on top of the cmplog stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog uses only havoc+token plus I2S, with no structural recombination.
value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile on the I2S delta (8/10 vs 8 blocked).
I2SRandReplace scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace mutator.
value_profile_cmplog beats plain cmplog on the value_profile delta (8/10 vs 2/10).
The CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets and preserves inputs that bring operands closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The winning grimoire seed contains a complete DOCTYPE/SYSTEM dtd reference structure verbatim.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness isolating the name3 dict-interned identity check where value_profile_cmplog and value_profile resolve at equal rates (no I2S advantage), and where grimoire shows no structural advantage over cmplog, would refute the multi-feature attribution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The I2S hypothesis is unverified in lineage because no vpc winning seed was bisected for this branch, so the I2S-vs-CMP_MAP split between Pairs 4 and 5 rests on mechanism reasoning rather than seed-byte evidence.

signature.mechanism_summary: AFLFast FAST n_fuzz rarity damping throttles already-exercised attribute-validation buckets, depleting mutation budget before any seed reaches val!=0; minimizer's intrinsic-calibration-only energy avoids this throttling.
signature.one_line: AFLFast n_fuzz damping depletes budget before val!=0 is reached

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute value reaching valid.c:3807 with a non-zero trailing char, where corpus-energy allocation (not byte content) gates discovery.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD attribute declaration whose value is parsed as NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION (xmlValidateAttributeValueInternal cases XML_ATTRIBUTE_NMTOKENS/XML_ATTRIBUTE_ENUMERATION at valid.c:3875) so xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal is invoked, AND whose value string, after the name-char and 0x20-blank loops, ends on a non-zero character so that valid.c:3807 `if (val != 0)` evaluates TRUE. The required input is structural (valid XML+DTD prefix with an ENUMERATION/NMTOKENS attribute), not a specific magic literal; both arms share the same XML/DTD scaffold bytes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Minimizer reaches the blocker's true side (L3807 W T=12 F=0) and resolves it in 9/10 trials. Its calibrated power schedule (PowerSchedule None, intrinsic-calibration-only energy) keeps allocating havoc/token mutations to the seeds carrying the XML+DTD ENUMERATION scaffold, so it eventually mutates a value that lands val!=0 at line 3807.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Fast is byte-for-byte the same fuzzer except for the AFLFast FAST power schedule; it blocks 8/10 (loser blocked=8, L3807 L T=0 F=90 — never takes the true side). The FAST n_fuzz-based energy formula damps seeds whose coverage bucket has been hit campaign-wide, starving the deep attribute-validation path of mutation budget before val!=0 is produced.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is enabled by minimizer's calibrated energy schedule (PowerSchedule None: per-seed mutation budget derived only from intrinsic calibration perf_score). The loser fast applies the AFLFast n_fuzz rarity damping that throttles already-exercised buckets, so the single-technique delta (aflfast_rarity) costs fast the mutation budget needed to push a seed to val!=0 at valid.c:3807. This is a scheduling/energy divergence, not comparison-solving or byte-content.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the true branch of valid.c:3807 `if (val != 0) return(0)`.
The enclosing validator is invoked only for NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute types via the caller switch.
Both winner and loser seeds carry the same XML+DTD scaffold (version/DOCTYPE/SYSTEM dtds prefix), so the differentiating feature is not a magic literal.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side while the loser never does (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L3807  T=12 F=0  T=0 F=90  if (val != 0) return(0);  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Minimizer resolves the branch in 9/10 trials and fast blocks in 8/10.
Fast is identical to minimizer except for the FAST power schedule; the single-technique delta is the scheduler.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore `fast`
vs `minimizer`, not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: The FAST schedule reweights per-seed mutation budget by n_fuzz, damping saturated paths and boosting rare ones.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Rarely-hit
paths get up to 4× energy; saturated paths are damped to 0.4×.

evidence_trail.claim: Minimizer's energy uses only intrinsic calibration and is not weighted by region hit-frequency.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
PowerSchedule is `None`, so the
energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often
a region has been hit.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating valid.c:3807 reached via the ENUMERATION/NMTOKENS path where fast resolves the branch as often as minimizer across trials, showing the AFLFast rarity damping does not starve this path and that the divergence was byte-content (not energy) driven.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from the FAST n_fuzz damping to this specific deep attribute-validation branch is inferred from the scheduler description plus shared-scaffold seeds; no per-seed n_fuzz/energy trace is present in the prompt to prove the path was actually starved rather than blocked by some byte feature I cannot see in the truncated diff.
